title: hi everyone
wikitext:
[]: hi everyone             Quote Post by amandawinnlee » Wed Sep 21, 2011 4:28 pm Hey Kids! This is Amanda Winn Lee. I just wanted to jump on your board and clear the air about a few things. I understand that both my commentary and my choice of sound effects in the End of Eva have raised a lot of hackles in the fan community. I want to apologize to those people. I understand that this is a very important work to you, and I honestly didn't mean to belittle you or the show. I handle all life situations with humor. (For example, when my son was getting treated for his cancer, I used to call him "chemo-sabe." Kind of sick, huh?) That doesn't mean I wasn't dedicated to bringing what I interpreted to be the true intent behind Eva to an American audience. During the production of the movies I was hospitalized for a week at one point during the post-production for a combination kidney infection and extreme physical exhaustion. I ran myself ragged examining many different interpretations and agonizing over each line because I know the show is so important to so many people. Eva became my life and I was determined to try and do it justice. Some will argue I did, other will say my dub was an abortion. I get that. But please do not make assume to know me or my personality when I have never even MET you. Criticize my work, absolutely. Saying that I as a human being have no reason to exist or that I'm an ignorant moron is uncalled for. Thanks! amanda

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Sep 21, 2011 4:42 pm ...We are actually making a new Fandub... if you're actually interested... and actually Amanda Winn Lee... I think this forum would rather prefer that you post some actual proof that you are who you say you are. Perhaps a small, URL only video introducing yourself to the community? Or something of the sort.

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Wed Sep 21, 2011 5:11 pm is this for real? is this really her or someone just pretending to be her (If this is really you sorry for my scepticism.)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 21, 2011 5:26 pm I have to concur -- extraordinary claims and extraordinary evidence, and all that jazz. For the moment I'll lock these threads to try to avert any unpleasantness from the peanut gallery.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Sep 22, 2011 1:43 pm Reasonable credentials have been established. Now, be civil, boys and girls.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Sep 22, 2011 1:55 pm One of the very few western anime personalities who got an entry in the Japanese wikipedia. Amanda, sorry I have not heard of your name until very recently (I spent most of my anime life in Asia/Japan, watching the Evangelion TV live back in 1995 and End of Eva on big screen in 1997) but I want to give my kudos to your achievement. And welcome to to this forum! Japanese wiki entry

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Sep 22, 2011 1:59 pm We welcome you, Amanda.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:06 pm welcome to the forum .

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:25 pm Well, if Tines trusts that this is legit then I guess it's worth believing. Guess this is a unique opportunity so I'll ask a question: During the dubbing process, did you come into contact with Anno? If so, did he ever tell you anything interesting about Eva that fans may not have heard before or may find useful to know?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:30 pm Now that we have Amanda Winn Lee, we just need to get Robin Williams to fess up as a member and we'll be complete!

[]:             Quote Post by amandawinnlee » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:36 pm I never met Anno. I sent my stuff to manga and they communicated with Gainax. I know some people hate the sound fx I added. Here's the thing- that's what I was hired to do. The original soundtrack only came in stereo, so they asked me to build the surround sound. Gainax had final say over everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:48 pm I foresee an epic shitstorm. @AWL herself . Cool, it's always nice to see people who have a firm grasp on fan-feedback. Um... I didn't really like your Rei... sorry. (But I mostly feel extreme apathy for the Eva dub anyway.) The EoE DVD menu for the Manga release is really cool though. I don't know if you had anything to do with it, but if you did, I like it.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:53 pm I enjoy the dub. Thanks for your work in bringing Evangelion to the English-speaking world, Amanda :) Sachi wrote: Now that we have Amanda Winn Lee, we just need to get Robin Williams to fess up as a member and we'll be complete!

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:54 pm Wow. So it's official, you're really on the site. :) Then I wonder whose next to join the site? Tiffany Grant? Spike Spencer? The list goes on. So, during your time working on Evangelion, did you have any contact with your Japanese equivalent, Megumi Hayashibara? If so, do you still keep in contact?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:54 pm Yay! Another three hours into the night of me and others PMing you in order to get evidence! Awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Sep 22, 2011 3:01 pm Welcome to EGF, Amanda. (I apologize for any past comments I have made that came across as rude. Fans can be a finicky bunch! ) Personally I don't like dubs, but I also enjoyed the DVD menus for both Death/Rebirth and EoE. They're a nice touch of surreal and chilling. Please enjoy your stay!

[]:             Quote Post by amandawinnlee » Thu Sep 22, 2011 3:17 pm You guys are sweet to try and soften your blows. :) I had nothing to do with the menu, that was all Manga. I know my voice/ directing choices aren't everyone's cup of tea, and I am not at all offended if you don't like my stuff. Art is subjective, so what one person adores, another loathes. It's when you guys start calling me an "attention whore" or "evil" or any of those other charming epithets that I take exception. I have never even met you, so saying those things is just mean spirited and ignorant. Remember to separate the work from the person.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Sep 22, 2011 3:33 pm I've been a rather vocal hater of the dub in the past. However, I have to give AWL huge props for coming on board and welcoming a discussion with her. I have two questions: 1) How was it that the entire English dub team confused a slap for a gunshot? 2) Did anyone notice that the word Evangelion was not being consistently pronounced from actor to actor? Oh and also... 3) Can you let the voice actor for Black Hyuga know he is a god around here?

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Thu Sep 22, 2011 3:33 pm This is actually pretty awesome. I'll admit that there are some decisions made in the dub that weren't my cup of tea (that I've mentioned on this forum before), but overall, I felt it was more or less in spirit with the ADV dub, and the series in general. And if nothing else, it gave the world Black!Hyuga, which is always an endless source of amusement for me. And personally, I do find the commentary amusing. A bit irksome at points, yes, but definitely worth quite a few good laughs (perhaps its just because I'm a sucker for puns, which it is rife with. XP) Given this rare opportunity, I do have to ask you, Amanda (if I may call you Amanda), a question that I have wondered about for quite some time. In the two EVA movies I noticed from my very first viewing that the word "EVA" is pronounced in an unusual way throughout (the "E" is more of an "eh" than the "ay"-like sound from the series). I've always wanted to know if there was a particular reason for that, seeing as how every character pronounces it that way. And, at any rate, welcome to the forums.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Sep 22, 2011 3:34 pm amandawinnlee wrote: You guys are sweet to try and soften your blows. :) I had nothing to do with the menu, that was all Manga. I know my voice/ directing choices aren't everyone's cup of tea, and I am not at all offended if you don't like my stuff. Art is subjective, so what one person adores, another loathes. It's when you guys start calling me an "attention whore" or "evil" or any of those other charming epithets that I take exception. I have never even met you, so saying those things is just mean spirited and ignorant. Remember to separate the work from the person.

[]: hi everyone             Quote Post by amandawinnlee » Wed Sep 21, 2011 4:28 pm Hey Kids! This is Amanda Winn Lee. I just wanted to jump on your board and clear the air about a few things. I understand that both my commentary and my choice of sound effects in the End of Eva have raised a lot of hackles in the fan community. I want to apologize to those people. I understand that this is a very important work to you, and I honestly didn't mean to belittle you or the show. I handle all life situations with humor. (For example, when my son was getting treated for his cancer, I used to call him "chemo-sabe." Kind of sick, huh?) That doesn't mean I wasn't dedicated to bringing what I interpreted to be the true intent behind Eva to an American audience. During the production of the movies I was hospitalized for a week at one point during the post-production for a combination kidney infection and extreme physical exhaustion. I ran myself ragged examining many different interpretations and agonizing over each line because I know the show is so important to so many people. Eva became my life and I was determined to try and do it justice. Some will argue I did, other will say my dub was an abortion. I get that. But please do not make assume to know me or my personality when I have never even MET you. Criticize my work, absolutely. Saying that I as a human being have no reason to exist or that I'm an ignorant moron is uncalled for. Thanks! amanda
